query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.50 (commit: b0b93f05).

## Issue to Resolve

Unexpected indentation level after newline with 0.50.0
With version 0.50.0, the below code now produces an error because of the lack of newline after the {:
```
{ size -> icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await() }
```
What seems odd to me is that the "correct" formatting looks like the below:
```
{
        size ->
    icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await()
}
```
This plays out the same for larger code blocks as well where the first line after the bracket is indented further over than all subsequent ones if there's a separator involved. It seems to me that if anything, we'd want to invert that indentation to something more like:
```
{
    size ->
        icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await()
}
```

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

This problem has the same root cause as #2106 and will be fixed via that issue.

The double indentation of parameters inside the lambda looks indeed weird at first sight. It is based on (but not identical to) the default formatting of IntelliJ IDEA.

Given the functional literal below (note that it contains multiple parameters on separate lines):
```
val foo = { a,
        b,
        c ->
    doSomething()
}
```
It is formatted by IntelliJ IDEA formatter as:
```
val foo = { a,
            b,
            c ->
    doSomething()
}
```
Analog to formatting of functions having parameters on separate lines, ktlint formats this as:
```
val foo = {
        a,
        b,
        c ->
    doSomething()
}
```

The format above should only be applied when having a function literal with parameters on separate lines. As result of the bug in #2101, your parameter list was wrapped and as of that changed into a multiline parameter list. As a result of that, the indentation of a multiline parameter list was applied. With fix of #2106 you original code sample will be kept unchanged.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/WrappingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1â†’# Changelog
  2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4â†’
  5â†’## Unreleased
  6â†’
  7â†’### Added
  8â†’
  9â†’### Removed
 10â†’
 11â†’### Fixed
 12â†’
 13â†’* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115) 
 14â†’
 15â†’### Changed
 16â†’
 17â†’* Update dependency gradle to v8.2 ([#2105](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2105))
 18â†’
 19â†’## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
 20â†’
 21â†’### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
 22â†’
 23â†’The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
 24â†’
 25â†’API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
 26â†’
 27â†’The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
 28â†’
 29â†’### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
 30â†’
 31â†’In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
 32â†’
 33â†’In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
 34â†’
 35â†’Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
 36â†’
 37â†’* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
 38â†’* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
 39â†’* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
 40â†’
 41â†’Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
 42â†’
 43â†’In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
 44â†’
 45â†’At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
 46â†’
 47â†’At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
 48â†’
 49â†’### Added
 50â†’
 51â†’* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
 52â†’* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)
 53â†’* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
 54â†’* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
 55â†’* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))
 56â†’* Wrap multiple statements on same line `wrapping` ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
 57â†’* Add new rule `ktlint-suppression` to replace the `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives with annotations. This rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` ([#1947](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1947))
 58â†’* Inform user about using `--format` option of KtLint CLI when finding a violation that can be autocorrected ([#1071](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1071))
 59â†’
 60â†’### Removed
 61â†’
 62â†’* Code which was deprecated in `0.49.x` is removed. Consult changelog of 0.49.x` released for more information. Summary of removed code: 
 63â†’
 64â†’### Fixed
 65â†’
 66â†’* Do not flag a (potential) mutable extension property in case the getter is annotated or prefixed with a modifier `property-naming` ([#2024](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2024))
 67â†’* Do not merge an annotated expression body with the function signature even if it fits on a single line ([#2043](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2043))
 68â†’* Ignore property with name `serialVersionUID` in `property-naming` ([#2045](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2045))
 69â†’* Prevent incorrect reporting of violations in case a nullable function type reference exceeds the maximum line length `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1324](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1324)) 
 70â†’* Prevent false negative on `else` branch when body contains only chained calls or binary expression ([#2057](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2057))
 71â†’* Fix indent when property value is wrapped to next line ([#2095](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2095)) 
 72â†’
 73â†’### Changed
 74â†’
 75â†’* Fix Java interoperability issues with `RuleId` and `RuleSetId` classes. Those classes were defined as value classes in `0.49.0` and `0.49.1`. Although the classes were marked with `@JvmInline` it seems that it is not possible to uses those classes from Java base API Consumers like Spotless. The classes have now been replaced with data classes ([#2041](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2041))
 76â†’* Update dependency `info.picocli:picocli` to v4.7.4
 77â†’* Update dependency `org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter` to v5.9.3
 78â†’* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.22` and Kotlin version to `1.8.22`.
 79â†’* Update dependency io.github.detekt.sarif4k:sarif4k to v0.4.0
 80â†’* Update dependency org.jetbrains.dokka:dokka-gradle-plugin to v1.8.20
 81â†’* Run format up to 3 times in case formatting introduces changes which also can be autocorrected ([#2084](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2084))
 82â†’
 83â†’## [0.49.1] - 2023-05-12
 84â†’
 85â†’### Added
 86â†’
 87â†’### Removed
 88â†’
 89â†’### Fixed
 90â†’* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
 91â†’* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
 92â†’* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
 93â†’* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
 94â†’* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
 95â†’* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if or operator at start of line is followed by dot qualified expression `indent` ([#1993](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1993))
 96â†’* Fix indentation of multiline parameter list in function literal `indent` ([#1976](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1976))
 97â†’* Restrict indentation of closing quotes to `ktlint_official` code style to keep formatting of other code styles consistent with `0.48.x` and before `indent` ([#1971](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1971))
 98â†’* Extract rule `no-single-line-block-comment` from `comment-wrapping` rule. The `no-single-line-block-comment` rule is added as experimental rule to the `ktlint_official` code style, but it can be enabled explicitly for the other code styles as well. ([#1980](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1980))
 99â†’* Clean-up unwanted logging dependencies ([#1998](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1998))
100â†’* Fix directory traversal for patterns referring to paths outside of current working directory or any of it child directories ([#2002](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2002))

  2â†’

 90â†’/**
 91â†’ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 92â†’ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 93â†’ *
 94â†’ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 95â†’ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 96â†’ * indentation rule is not run.
 97â†’ */
 98â†’public class WrappingRule :
 99â†’    StandardRule(
100â†’        id = "wrapping",
101â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties =
102â†’            setOf(
103â†’                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
104â†’                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
105â†’                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
106â†’            ),
107â†’        visitorModifiers =
108â†’            setOf(
109â†’                RunAfterRule(
110â†’                    ruleId = ANNOTATION_RULE_ID,
111â†’                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
112â†’                ),
113â†’            ),
114â†’    ) {
115â†’    private var line = 1
116â†’    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
117â†’    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
118â†’
119â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
120â†’        line = 1
121â†’        indentConfig =
122â†’            IndentConfig(
123â†’                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
124â†’                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
125â†’            )
126â†’        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
127â†’    }
128â†’
129â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
130â†’        node: ASTNode,
131â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
132â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
133â†’    ) {
134â†’        when (node.elementType) {
135â†’            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
136â†’            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
137â†’            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
138â†’            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
139â†’            TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST, TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> rearrangeTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
140â†’            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
141â†’            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
142â†’            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
143â†’            SEMICOLON -> insertNewLineAfterSemi(node, autoCorrect, emit)
144â†’        }
145â†’    }
146â†’
147â†’    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
148â†’        node: ASTNode,
149â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
150â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
151â†’    ) {
152â†’        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
153â†’
154â†’        val lbrace =
155â†’            node
156â†’                .getStartOfBlock()
157â†’                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == LBRACE }
158â†’                ?: return
159â†’        if (lbrace.followedByNewline() ||
160â†’            lbrace.followedByEolComment() ||
161â†’            lbrace.isPartOf(LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY)
162â†’        ) {
163â†’            // String template inside raw string literal may exceed the maximum line length
164â†’            return
165â†’        }
166â†’
167â†’        node
168â†’            .takeUnless { it.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
169â†’            ?.getEndOfBlock()
170â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == RBRACE }
171â†’            ?.let { rbrace ->
172â†’                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(lbrace, rbrace)) {
173â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(lbrace, autoCorrect, emit)
174â†’                }
175â†’            }
176â†’
177â†’        if (maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF) {
178â†’            val lengthUntilBeginOfLine =
179â†’                node
180â†’                    .leaves(false)
181â†’                    .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
182â†’                    .sumOf { it.textLength }
183â†’            val lengthUntilEndOfLine =
184â†’                node
185â†’                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
186â†’                    .leavesIncludingSelf()
187â†’                    .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
188â†’                    .sumOf { it.textLength }
189â†’            if (lengthUntilBeginOfLine + lengthUntilEndOfLine > maxLineLength) {
190â†’                requireNewlineAfterLeaf(lbrace, autoCorrect, emit)
191â†’            }
192â†’        }
193â†’    }
194â†’
195â†’    private fun ASTNode.followedByEolComment() =
196â†’        null !=
197â†’            leaves()
198â†’                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
199â†’                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
200â†’
201â†’    private fun rearrangeBlock(
202â†’        node: ASTNode,
203â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
204â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
205â†’    ) {
206â†’        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
207â†’        var newlineInBetween = false
208â†’        var parameterListInBetween = false
209â†’        var numberOfArgs = 0
210â†’        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
211â†’        // matching ), ] or }
212â†’        val r = node.nextSibling {
213â†’            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
214â†’            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
215â†’            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
216â†’            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
217â†’            if (isValueArgument) {
218â†’                numberOfArgs++
219â†’                firstArg = it
220â†’            }
221â†’            it.elementType == rElementType
222â†’        }!!
223â†’        if (
224â†’            !newlineInBetween ||
225â†’            // keep { p ->
226â†’            // }
227â†’            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
228â†’            // keep ({
229â†’            // }) and (object : C {
230â†’            // })
231â†’            (
232â†’                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
233â†’                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
234â†’                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
235â†’                )
236â†’        ) {
237â†’            return
238â†’        }
239â†’        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
240â†’            // keep:
241â†’            // for (foo in listOf(
242â†’            //     "foo-1",
243â†’            //     "foo-2"
244â†’            // )) { ... }
245â†’            // but reject:
246â†’            // for (
247â†’            //     foo in listOf(
248â†’            //         "foo-1",
249â†’            //         "foo-2"
250â†’            //     )
251â†’            // ) { ... }
252â†’            return
253â†’        }
254â†’        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
255â†’                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
256â†’                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
257â†’                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
258â†’                    it.textLength > 0
259â†’            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
260â†’            // IDEA quirk:
261â†’            // if (true &&
262â†’            //     true
263â†’            // ) {
264â†’            // }
265â†’            // instead of
266â†’            // if (
267â†’            //     true &&
268â†’            //     true
269â†’            // ) {
270â†’            // }
271â†’            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
272â†’        ) {
273â†’            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
274â†’        }
275â†’        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
276â†’            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node))
277â†’        }
278â†’    }
279â†’
280â†’    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
281â†’        node: ASTNode,
282â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
283â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
284â†’    ) {
285â†’        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
286â†’        if (
287â†’            node.textContains('\n') &&
288â†’            entries.size > 1 &&
289â†’            // e.g.
290â†’            //
291â†’            // class A : B, C,
292â†’            //     D
293â†’            // or
294â†’            // class A : B, C({
295â†’            // }), D
296â†’            //
297â†’            // but not
298â†’            //
299â†’            // class A : B, C, D({
300â†’            // })
301â†’            !(
302â†’                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
303â†’                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
304â†’                )
305â†’        ) {
306â†’            // put space after :
307â†’            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
308â†’                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
309â†’                if (
310â†’                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
311â†’                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
312â†’                ) {
313â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
314â†’                }
315â†’            }
316â†’            // put entries on separate lines
317â†’            for (c in node.children()) {
318â†’                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
319â†’                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
320â†’                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
321â†’                ) {
322â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
323â†’                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
324â†’                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
325â†’                        emit = emit,
326â†’                        indent = node.indent(),
327â†’                    )
328â†’                }
329â†’            }
330â†’        }
331â†’    }
332â†’
333â†’    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
334â†’        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
335â†’            ?.isPartOfComment() == true
336â†’
337â†’    private fun rearrangeValueList(
338â†’        node: ASTNode,
339â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
340â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
341â†’    ) {
342â†’        for (c in node.children()) {
343â†’            val hasLineBreak =
344â†’                when (c.elementType) {
345â†’                    VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
346â†’                    VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
347â†’                    else -> false
348â†’                }
349â†’            if (hasLineBreak) {
350â†’                // rearrange
351â†’                //
352â†’                // a, b, value(
353â†’                // ), c, d
354â†’                //
355â†’                // to
356â†’                //
357â†’                // a, b,
358â†’                // value(
359â†’                // ),
360â†’                // c, d
361â†’
362â†’                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
363â†’                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
364â†’                if (
365â†’                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
366â†’                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
367â†’                ) {
368â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
369â†’                }
370â†’                // insert \n after multi-line value
371â†’                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
372â†’                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
373â†’                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
374â†’                if (
375â†’                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
376â†’                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
377â†’                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
378â†’                    // value(
379â†’                    // ), // a comment
380â†’                    // c, d
381â†’                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
382â†’                ) {
383â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
384â†’                }
385â†’            }
386â†’        }
387â†’    }
388â†’
389â†’    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
390â†’        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
391â†’            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
392â†’
393â†’    private fun rearrangeTypeArgumentList(
394â†’        node: ASTNode,
395â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
396â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
397â†’    ) {
398â†’        if (node.textContains('\n')) {
399â†’            // Each type projection must be preceded with a whitespace containing a newline
400â†’            node
401â†’                .children()
402â†’                .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION || it.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER }
403â†’                .forEach { typeProjection ->
404â†’                    typeProjection
405â†’                        .prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
406â†’                        .let { prevSibling ->
407â†’                            if (prevSibling?.elementType == LT || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
408â†’                                emit(typeProjection.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${typeProjection.text}'", true)
409â†’                                if (autoCorrect) {
410â†’                                    typeProjection.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
411â†’                                }
412â†’                            }
413â†’                        }
414â†’                }
415â†’
416â†’            // After the last type projection a whitespace containing a newline must exist
417â†’            node
418â†’                .findChildByType(GT)
419â†’                ?.let { closingAngle ->
420â†’                    val prevSibling = closingAngle.prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
421â†’                    if (prevSibling?.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
422â†’                        emit(closingAngle.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${closingAngle.text}'", true)
423â†’                        if (autoCorrect) {
424â†’                            closingAngle.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
425â†’                        }
426â†’                    }
427â†’                }
428â†’
429â†’            Unit
430â†’        }
431â†’    }
432â†’
433â†’    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
434â†’        node: ASTNode,
435â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
436â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
437â†’    ) {
438â†’        node
439â†’            .treeParent
440â†’            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
441â†’            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
442â†’            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
443â†’            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
444â†’            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
445â†’            ?.let {
446â†’                // rewriting
447â†’                // """
448â†’                //     text
449â†’                // _""".trimIndent()
450â†’                // to
451â†’                // """
452â†’                //     text
453â†’                // _
454â†’                // """.trimIndent()
455â†’                emit(
456â†’                    node.startOffset,
457â†’                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
458â†’                    true,
459â†’                )
460â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
461â†’                    node as LeafPsiElement
462â†’                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
463â†’                }
464â†’                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
465â†’            }
466â†’    }
467â†’
468â†’    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
469â†’        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
470â†’        // if token is in lTokenSet
471â†’        //     find matching rToken
472â†’        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
473â†’        // return false
474â†’        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
475â†’        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
476â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
477â†’            // special cases:
478â†’            // x = y.f({ z ->
479â†’            // })
480â†’            // x = y.f(0, 1,
481â†’            // 2, 3)
482â†’            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
483â†’        }
484â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
485â†’            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
486â†’            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
487â†’            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
488â†’        }
489â†’        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
490â†’            return true
491â†’        }
492â†’        return false
493â†’    }
494â†’
495â†’    private fun rearrangeArrow(
496â†’        node: ASTNode,
497â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
498â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
499â†’    ) {
500â†’        val p = node.treeParent
501â†’        if (
502â†’            // check
503â†’            // `{ p -> ... }`
504â†’            // and
505â†’            // `when { m -> ... }`
506â†’            // only
507â†’            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
508â†’            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
509â†’            !p.textContains('\n') ||
510â†’            // permit
511â†’            // when {
512â†’            //     m -> 0 + d({
513â†’            //     })
514â†’            // }
515â†’            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
516â†’            // permit
517â†’            // when (this) {
518â†’            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
519â†’            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
520â†’            //     -> true
521â†’            // }
522â†’            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
523â†’        ) {
524â†’            return
525â†’        }
526â†’        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
527â†’            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
528â†’        }
529â†’        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
530â†’        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
531â†’            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.indent())
532â†’        }
533â†’    }
534â†’
535â†’    private fun insertNewLineAfterSemi(
536â†’        node: ASTNode,
537â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
538â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
539â†’    ) {
540â†’        val previousCodeLeaf = node.prevCodeLeaf()?.lastChildLeafOrSelf() ?: return
541â†’        val nextCodeLeaf = node.nextCodeLeaf()?.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ?: return
542â†’        if (previousCodeLeaf.treeParent.elementType == ENUM_ENTRY && nextCodeLeaf.elementType == RBRACE) {
543â†’            // Allow
544â†’            // enum class INDEX2 { ONE, TWO, THREE; }
545â†’            return
546â†’        }
547â†’        if (noNewLineInClosedRange(previousCodeLeaf, nextCodeLeaf)) {
548â†’            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit, previousCodeLeaf.indent())
549â†’            node
550â†’                .treeParent
551â†’                .takeIf { it.elementType == BLOCK }
552â†’                ?.let { block ->
553â†’                    beforeVisitBlock(block, autoCorrect, emit)
554â†’                    block
555â†’                        .treeParent
556â†’                        .takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
557â†’                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
558â†’                        ?.let { arrow -> rearrangeArrow(arrow, autoCorrect, emit) }
559â†’                }
560â†’        }
561â†’    }
562â†’
563â†’    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
564â†’        node: ASTNode,
565â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
566â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
567â†’        indent: String,
568â†’    ) {
569â†’        emit(
570â†’            node.startOffset - 1,
571â†’            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
572â†’            true,
573â†’        )
574â†’        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
575â†’        if (autoCorrect) {
576â†’            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indent)
577â†’        }
578â†’    }
579â†’
580â†’    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
581â†’        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
582â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
583â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
584â†’        indent: String? = null,
585â†’        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
586â†’    ) {
587â†’        emit(
588â†’            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
589â†’            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
590â†’            true,
591â†’        )
592â†’        LOGGER.trace {
593â†’            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
594â†’        }
595â†’        if (autoCorrect) {
596â†’            val tempIndent = indent ?: (indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
597â†’            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(tempIndent)
598â†’        }
599â†’    }
600â†’
601â†’    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
602â†’        for (child in node.children()) {
603â†’            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
604â†’                val v = child.text
605â†’                if (v == "\n") {
606â†’                    return true
607â†’                }
608â†’            }
609â†’        }
610â†’        return false
611â†’    }
612â†’
613â†’    private fun ASTNode.hasLineBreak(vararg ignoreElementTypes: IElementType): Boolean {
614â†’        if (isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return true
615â†’        return if (ignoreElementTypes.isEmpty()) {
616â†’            textContains('\n')
617â†’        } else {
618â†’            elementType !in ignoreElementTypes &&
619â†’                children().any { c -> c.textContains('\n') && c.elementType !in ignoreElementTypes }
620â†’        }
621â†’    }
622â†’
623â†’    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
624â†’
625â†’    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
626â†’
627â†’    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
628â†’        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
629â†’            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
630â†’
631â†’    /**
632â†’     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
633â†’     *     for (foo in listOf(
634â†’     *         "foo-1",
635â†’     *         "foo-2"
636â†’     *     )) { ... }
637â†’     * but reject:
638â†’     *     for (
639â†’     *         foo in listOf(
640â†’     *             "foo-1",
641â†’     *             "foo-2"
642â†’     *         )
643â†’     *     ) { ... }
644â†’     */
645â†’    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
646â†’        if (treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FOR) {
647â†’            return false
648â†’        }
649â†’        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
650â†’            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
651â†’        }
652â†’        require(elementType == LPAR) {
653â†’            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
654â†’        }
655â†’
656â†’        var node: ASTNode? = this
657â†’        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
658â†’            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
659â†’                return false
660â†’            }
661â†’            node = node.nextSibling()
662â†’        }
663â†’        return true
664â†’    }
665â†’
666â†’    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
667â†’        node: ASTNode,
668â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
669â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
670â†’    ) {
671â†’        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
672â†’            val lbrace =
673â†’                node
674â†’                    .getStartOfBlock()
675â†’                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == LBRACE }
676â†’                    ?: return
677â†’            node
678â†’                .getEndOfBlock()
679â†’                ?.takeUnless { it.isPrecededByNewline() }
680â†’                ?.let { rbrace ->
681â†’                    if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(lbrace, rbrace)) {
682â†’                        requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
683â†’                            rbrace,
684â†’                            autoCorrect,
685â†’                            emit,
686â†’                            indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node),
687â†’                        )
688â†’                    }
689â†’                }
690â†’        }
691â†’    }
692â†’
693â†’    private fun ASTNode.followedByNewline() = nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
694â†’
695â†’    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByNewline() = prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
696â†’
697â†’    private fun ASTNode.getStartOfBlock() =
698â†’        firstChildLeafOrSelf()
699â†’            .let { node ->
700â†’                if (node.elementType == LBRACE) {
701â†’                    // WHEN-entry block have LBRACE and RBRACE as first and last elements
702â†’                    node
703â†’                } else {
704â†’                    // Other blocks have LBRACE and RBRACE as siblings of the block
705â†’                    node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
706â†’                }
707â†’            }
708â†’
709â†’    private fun ASTNode.getEndOfBlock() =
710â†’        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
711â†’            .let { node ->
712â†’                if (node.elementType == RBRACE && treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
713â†’                    // WHEN-entry block have LBRACE and RBRACE as first and last elements
714â†’                    node
715â†’                } else {
716â†’                    // Other blocks have LBRACE and RBRACE as siblings of the block
717â†’                    node.nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
718â†’                }
719â†’            }
720â†’
721â†’    private companion object {
722â†’        private val LTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(LPAR, LBRACE, LBRACKET, LT)
723â†’        private val RTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(RPAR, RBRACE, RBRACKET, GT)
724â†’        private val MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP =
725â†’            LTOKEN_SET.types.zip(
726â†’                RTOKEN_SET.types,
727â†’            ).toMap()
728â†’    }
729â†’}
730â†’
731â†’public val WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = WrappingRule().ruleId
732â†’

content.text: Let me look at more of the CHANGELOG to find references to issue #2106 and also explore the IndentationRule since this issue is about indentation:

 129â†’public class IndentationRule :
 130â†’    StandardRule(
 131â†’        id = "indent",
 132â†’        visitorModifiers =
 133â†’            setOf(
 134â†’                VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 135â†’                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 136â†’                    ruleId = FUNCTION_SIGNATURE_RULE_ID,

 140â†’                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_RULE_ID,
 141â†’                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 142â†’                ),
 143â†’                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 144â†’                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID,
 145â†’                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 146â†’                ),
 147â†’            ),
 148â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 149â†’            setOf(
 150â†’                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 151â†’                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 152â†’                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 153â†’            ),
 154â†’    ) {
 155â†’    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 156â†’    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 157â†’
 158â†’    private var line = 1
 159â†’
 160â†’    private val indentContextStack: Deque<IndentContext> = LinkedList()
 161â†’
 162â†’    private lateinit var stringTemplateIndenter: StringTemplateIndenter
 163â†’
 164â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 165â†’        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 166â†’        indentConfig =
 167â†’            IndentConfig(
 168â†’                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 169â†’                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 170â†’            )
 171â†’        if (indentConfig.disabled) {
 172â†’            stopTraversalOfAST()
 173â†’        }
 174â†’    }
 175â†’
 176â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 177â†’        node: ASTNode,
 178â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 179â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 180â†’    ) {
 181â†’        if (node.isRoot()) {
 182â†’            // File should not start with a whitespace
 183â†’            node
 184â†’                .nextLeaf()
 185â†’                ?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 186â†’                ?.let { whitespaceWithoutNewline ->
 187â†’                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected indentation", true)
 188â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {
 189â†’                        whitespaceWithoutNewline.treeParent.removeChild(whitespaceWithoutNewline)
 190â†’                    }
 191â†’                }
 192â†’            indentContextStack.addLast(startNoIndentZone(node))
 193â†’        }
 194â†’
 195â†’        when {
 196â†’            node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() -> {
 197â†’                line++
 198â†’                if (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.activated == false) {
 199â†’                    val lastIndentContext = indentContextStack.removeLast()
 200â†’                    indentContextStack.addLast(
 201â†’                        lastIndentContext.copy(activated = true),
 202â†’                    )
 203â†’                }
 204â†’                visitNewLineIndentation(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 205â†’            }
 206â†’
 207â†’            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 208â†’                node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ||
 209â†’                node.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY ||
 210â†’                node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE ||
 211â†’                node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST ->
 212â†’                startIndentContext(
 213â†’                    fromAstNode = node,
 214â†’                    lastChildIndent = "",
 215â†’                )
 216â†’
 217â†’            node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY -> {
 218â†’                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isPartOfClassWithAMultilinePrimaryConstructor()) {
 219â†’                    // Contrary to the default IntelliJ IDEA formatter, indent the super type call entry so that it looks better in case it
 220â†’                    // is followed by another super type:
 221â†’                    //      class Foo(
 222â†’                    //          val bar1: Bar,
 223â†’                    //          val bar2: Bar,
 224â†’                    //      ) : FooBar(
 225â†’                    //              bar1,
 226â†’                    //              bar2
 227â†’                    //          ),
 228â†’                    //          BarFoo,
 229â†’                    startIndentContext(
 230â†’                        fromAstNode = node,
 231â†’                        childIndent = indentConfig.indent,
 232â†’                        activated = true,
 233â†’                    )
 234â†’                } else {
 235â†’                    startIndentContext(
 236â†’                        fromAstNode = node,
 237â†’                        lastChildIndent = "",
 238â†’                    )
 239â†’                }
 240â†’            }
 241â†’
 242â†’            node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
 243â†’                visitValueArgument(node)
 244â†’
 245â†’            node.elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR ->
 246â†’                visitSecondaryConstructor(node)
 247â†’
 248â†’            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED &&
 249â†’                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF ->
 250â†’                startIndentContext(node)
 251â†’
 252â†’            node.elementType == BINARY_WITH_TYPE ||
 253â†’                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 254â†’                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
 255â†’                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ||
 256â†’                node.elementType == USER_TYPE ->
 257â†’                startIndentContext(node)
 258â†’
 259â†’            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 260â†’                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
 261â†’                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->
 262â†’                startIndentContext(
 263â†’                    fromAstNode = node,
 264â†’                    childIndent = "",
 265â†’                )
 266â†’
 267â†’            node.elementType == IF ->
 268â†’                visitIf(node)
 269â†’
 270â†’            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
 271â†’                visitLbrace(node)
 272â†’
 273â†’            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
 274â†’                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
 275â†’                startIndentContext(
 276â†’                    fromAstNode = node,
 277â†’                    lastChildIndent = "",
 278â†’                )
 279â†’
 280â†’            node.elementType == LPAR &&
 281â†’                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
 282â†’                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
 283â†’
 284â†’            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
 285â†’                visitValueParameter(node)
 286â†’
 287â†’            node.elementType == FUN ->
 288â†’                visitFun(node)
 289â†’
 290â†’            node.elementType == CLASS ->
 291â†’                visitClass(node)
 292â†’
 293â†’            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->
 294â†’                visitObjectDeclaration(node)
 295â†’
 296â†’            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
 297â†’                visitBinaryExpression(node)
 298â†’
 299â†’            node.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION -> {
 300â†’                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
 301â†’                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
 302â†’                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&
 303â†’                    node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION
 304â†’                ) {
 305â†’                    // Issue 1540: Deviate and fix from incorrect formatting in IntelliJ IDEA formatting and produce following:
 306â†’                    // val fooBar2 = foo
 307â†’                    //    .bar[0] {
 308â†’                    //        "foobar"
 309â†’                    //    }
 310â†’                    startIndentContext(
 311â†’                        fromAstNode = node.treeParent,
 312â†’                        toAstNode = node.treeParent.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 313â†’                    )
 314â†’                } else if (node.prevCodeSibling().isElvisOperator()) {
 315â†’                    startIndentContext(node)
 316â†’                } else if (node.treeParent.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION) {
 317â†’                    // Multiple dot qualified expressions and/or safe expression on the same line should not increase the indent level
 318â†’                } else {
 319â†’                    startIndentContext(node)
 320â†’                }
 321â†’            }
 322â†’
 323â†’            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
 324â†’                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->
 325â†’                visitIdentifierInProperty(node)
 326â†’
 327â†’            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&
 328â†’                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE ->
 329â†’                visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 330â†’
 331â†’            node.elementType == WHEN ->
 332â†’                visitWhen(node)
 333â†’
 334â†’            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
 335â†’                visitWhenEntry(node)
 336â†’
 337â†’            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
 338â†’                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
 339â†’                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)
 340â†’
 341â†’            node.elementType == KDOC ->
 342â†’                visitKdoc(node)
 343â†’
 344â†’            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ||
 345â†’                node.elementType == TYPEALIAS ->
 346â†’                visitPropertyAccessor(node)
 347â†’
 348â†’            node.elementType == FOR ||
 349â†’                node.elementType == WHILE ->
 350â†’                visitConditionalLoop(node)
 351â†’
 352â†’            node.elementType == LBRACKET ->
 353â†’                visitLBracket(node)
 354â†’
 355â†’            node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE ->
 356â†’                visitNullableType(node)
 357â†’
 358â†’            node.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION ->
 359â†’                visitDestructuringDeclaration(node)
 360â†’
 361â†’            node.elementType == TRY ->
 362â†’                visitTryCatchFinally(node)
 363â†’
 364â†’            else -> {
 365â†’                LOGGER.trace { "No processing for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
 366â†’            }
 367â†’        }
 368â†’    }
 369â†’
 370â†’    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfClassWithAMultilinePrimaryConstructor() =
 371â†’        parent { it.elementType == CLASS }
 372â†’            ?.findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
 373â†’            ?.textContains('\n') == true
 374â†’
 375â†’    private fun visitValueArgument(node: ASTNode) {
 376â†’        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 377â†’            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 378â†’            //     val foo = foo(
 379â†’            //         parameterName =
 380â†’            //             "The quick brown fox "
 381â†’            //                .plus("jumps ")
 382â†’            //                .plus("over the lazy dog"),
 383â†’            //     )
 384â†’            startIndentContext(
 385â†’                fromAstNode = node,
 386â†’                lastChildIndent = "",
 387â†’            )
 388â†’        }
 389â†’    }

 391â†’    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
 392â†’        node
 393â†’            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
 394â†’            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
 395â†’                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 396â†’                val nextToAstNode =
 397â†’                    startIndentContext(
 398â†’                        fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
 399â†’                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 400â†’
 401â†’                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 402â†’                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
 403â†’                    startIndentContext(
 404â†’                        fromAstNode = node,
 405â†’                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 406â†’                        childIndent = "",
 407â†’                    )
 408â†’                }
 409â†’            }
 410â†’    }

 412â†’    private fun visitIf(node: ASTNode) {
 413â†’        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 414â†’        node
 415â†’            .findChildByType(ELSE)
 416â†’            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 417â†’                nextToAstNode =
 418â†’                    startIndentContext(
 419â†’                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 420â†’                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 421â†’                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 422â†’            }
 423â†’
 424â†’        node
 425â†’            .findChildByType(THEN)
 426â†’            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 427â†’            ?.nextLeaf()
 428â†’            ?.let { nodeAfterThenBlock ->
 429â†’                nextToAstNode =
 430â†’                    startIndentContext(
 431â†’                        fromAstNode = nodeAfterThenBlock,
 432â†’                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 433â†’                        childIndent = "",
 434â†’                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 435â†’            }
 436â†’        node
 437â†’            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 438â†’            ?.nextCodeLeaf()
 439â†’            ?.let { nodeAfterConditionBlock ->
 440â†’                nextToAstNode =
 441â†’                    startIndentContext(
 442â†’                        fromAstNode = nodeAfterConditionBlock,
 443â†’                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 444â†’                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 445â†’            }
 446â†’        startIndentContext(
 447â†’            fromAstNode = node,
 448â†’            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 449â†’            lastChildIndent = "", // No indent for the RPAR
 450â†’        )
 451â†’    }

 453â†’    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
 454â†’        // Outer indent context
 455â†’        val rbrace =
 456â†’            requireNotNull(
 457â†’                node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE },
 458â†’            ) { "Can not find matching rbrace" }
 459â†’        startIndentContext(
 460â†’            fromAstNode = node,
 461â†’            toAstNode = rbrace,
 462â†’            firstChildIndent = "",
 463â†’            lastChildIndent = "",
 464â†’        )
 465â†’
 466â†’        // Inner indent context in reversed order
 467â†’        node
 468â†’            .treeParent
 469â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
 470â†’            ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
 471â†’            ?.let { arrow ->
 472â†’                startIndentContext(
 473â†’                    fromAstNode = arrow,
 474â†’                    toAstNode = rbrace,
 475â†’                    lastChildIndent = "",
 476â†’                )
 477â†’                startIndentContext(
 478â†’                    fromAstNode = node,
 479â†’                    toAstNode = arrow.prevCodeLeaf()!!,
 480â†’                    childIndent = arrow.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters(),
 481â†’                )
 482â†’            }
 483â†’    }
 484â†’
 485â†’    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
 486â†’        if (isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
 487â†’            // val fieldExample =
 488â†’            //      LongNameClass {
 489â†’            //              paramA,
 490â†’            //              paramB,
 491â†’            //              paramC ->
 492â†’            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 493â†’            //      }
 494â†’            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 495â†’        } else {
 496â†’            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
 497â†’            // val fieldExample =
 498â†’            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
 499â†’            //                     paramB,
 500â†’            //                     paramC ->
 501â†’            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 502â†’            //     }
 503â†’            // val fieldExample =
 504â†’            //     someFunction(
 505â†’            //         1,
 506â†’            //         2,
 507â†’            //     ) { paramA,
 508â†’            //         paramB,
 509â†’            //         paramC ->
 510â†’            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 511â†’            //     }
 512â†’            this
 513â†’                .takeIf { it.isPartOf(CALL_EXPRESSION) }
 514â†’                ?.treeParent
 515â†’                ?.leaves(false)
 516â†’                ?.takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
 517â†’                ?.sumOf { it.textLength }
 518â†’                ?.plus(2) // need to add spaces to compensate for "{ "
 519â†’                ?.let { length -> " ".repeat(length) }
 520â†’                ?: indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 521â†’        }

 523â†’    private fun ASTNode.isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine() =
 524â†’        parent(FUNCTION_LITERAL)
 525â†’            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
 526â†’            ?.prevSibling { it.textContains('\n') } != null
 527â†’
 528â†’    private fun visitLparBeforeCondition(node: ASTNode) {
 529â†’        startIndentContext(
 530â†’            fromAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextLeaf()), // Allow to pickup whitespace before condition
 531â†’            toAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextCodeSibling()).lastChildLeafOrSelf(), // Ignore whitespace after condition but before rpar
 532â†’            nodeIndent = currentIndent() + indentConfig.indent,
 533â†’            childIndent = "",
 534â†’        )
 535â†’    }
 536â†’
 537â†’    private fun visitValueParameter(node: ASTNode) {
 538â†’        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 539â†’        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 540â†’        node
 541â†’            .findChildByType(EQ)
 542â†’            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 543â†’                nextToAstNode =
 544â†’                    startIndentContext(
 545â†’                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 546â†’                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 547â†’                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 548â†’            }
 549â†’
 550â†’        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 551â†’            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 552â†’            //     fun process(
 553â†’            //         aVariableWithAVeryLongName:
 554â†’            //             TypeWithAVeryLongNameThatDoesNotFitOnSameLineAsTheVariableName
 555â†’            //     ): List<Output>
 556â†’            node
 557â†’                .findChildByType(COLON)
 558â†’                ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 559â†’                    nextToAstNode =
 560â†’                        startIndentContext(
 561â†’                            fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 562â†’                            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 563â†’                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 564â†’                }
 565â†’        }
 566â†’
 567â†’        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 568â†’        val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 569â†’        if (fromAstNode != node.firstChildNode &&
 570â†’            node.prevSibling { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == null &&
 571â†’            node == node.treeParent.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER)
 572â†’        ) {
 573â†’            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 574â†’                fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 575â†’                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 576â†’            ).fromASTNode.prevLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpace() }!!
 577â†’        } else {
 578â†’            startIndentContext(
 579â†’                fromAstNode = node,
 580â†’                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 581â†’                childIndent = "",
 582â†’            )
 583â†’        }
 584â†’    }
 585â†’
 586â†’    private fun visitFun(node: ASTNode) {
 587â†’        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 588â†’        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 589â†’        val eqOrBlock =
 590â†’            node.findChildByType(EQ)
 591â†’                ?: node.findChildByType(BLOCK)
 592â†’        eqOrBlock?.let {
 593â†’            nextToAstNode =
 594â†’                startIndentContext(
 595â†’                    fromAstNode = eqOrBlock,
 596â†’                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 597â†’                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 598â†’        }
 599â†’
 600â†’        node
 601â†’            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
 602â†’            ?.let { typeReference ->
 603â†’                nextToAstNode =
 604â†’                    startIndentContext(
 605â†’                        fromAstNode = typeReference.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 606â†’                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 607â†’                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 608â†’            }
 609â†’
 610â†’        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as function itself
 611â†’        startIndentContext(
 612â†’            fromAstNode = node,
 613â†’            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 614â†’            childIndent = "",
 615â†’        )
 616â†’    }
 617â†’
 618â†’    private fun visitClass(node: ASTNode) {
 619â†’        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 620â†’        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 621â†’        node
 622â†’            .findChildByType(WHERE_KEYWORD)
 623â†’            ?.let { where ->
 624â†’                val typeConstraintList =
 625â†’                    requireNotNull(
 626â†’                        where.nextCodeSibling(),
 627â†’                    ) { "Can not find code sibling after WHERE in CLASS" }
 628â†’                require(
 629â†’                    typeConstraintList.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST,
 630â†’                ) { "Code sibling after WHERE in CLASS is not a TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST" }
 631â†’                nextToAstNode =
 632â†’                    startIndentContext(
 633â†’                        fromAstNode = where.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 634â†’                        toAstNode = typeConstraintList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 635â†’                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 636â†’            }
 637â†’
 638â†’        val primaryConstructor = node.findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
 639â†’        val containsConstructorKeyword = primaryConstructor?.findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_KEYWORD) != null
 640â†’        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && primaryConstructor != null && containsConstructorKeyword) {
 641â†’            // Contrary to the default IntelliJ IDEA formatter, ident both constructor and super type list as follows:
 642â†’            //     class Foo
 643â†’            //        @Bar1 @Bar2
 644â†’            //        constructor(
 645â†’            //            foo1: Foo1,
 646â†’            //            foo2: Foo2,
 647â†’            //        ) : Foobar1(
 648â†’            //                "foobar1",
 649â†’            //                "foobar2",
 650â†’            //            ),
 651â†’            //            FooBar2,
 652â†’            val superTypeList = node.findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 653â†’            nextToAstNode =
 654â†’                startIndentContext(
 655â†’                    fromAstNode = primaryConstructor.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 656â†’                    toAstNode =
 657â†’                        superTypeList
 658â†’                            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 659â†’                            ?: nextToAstNode,
 660â†’                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 661â†’
 662â†’            superTypeList
 663â†’                ?.findChildByType(COMMA)
 664â†’                ?.let { comma ->
 665â†’                    // In case of a multiline primary constructor the first super type is merged with the closing parenthesis of the
 666â†’                    // constructor. The start of the super type list does not activate the indent because it is not preceded by a newline.
 667â†’                    // To fix this, the super type entries on the next line (e.g. after the comma) have to be double indented.
 668â†’                    // Allow:
 669â†’                    //     class Foo(
 670â†’                    //         val bar1: Bar,
 671â†’                    //         val bar2: Bar,
 672â†’                    //     ) : FooBar(
 673â†’                    //             bar1,
 674â†’                    //             bar2
 675â†’                    //         ),
 676â†’                    //         BarFoo1
 677â†’                    val prevCodeLeaf =
 678â†’                        startIndentContext(
 679â†’                            fromAstNode = comma,
 680â†’                            toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 681â†’                            childIndent = indentConfig.indent.repeat(2),
 682â†’                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 683â†’                    startIndentContext(
 684â†’                        fromAstNode = primaryConstructor.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 685â†’                        toAstNode = prevCodeLeaf,
 686â†’                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 687â†’                }
 688â†’        } else {
 689â†’            node
 690â†’                .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 691â†’                ?.let { superTypeList ->
 692â†’                    nextToAstNode =
 693â†’                        startIndentContext(
 694â†’                            fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 695â†’                            toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 696â†’                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 697â†’                }
 698â†’        }
 699â†’
 700â†’        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 701â†’        startIndentContext(
 702â†’            fromAstNode = node,
 703â†’            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 704â†’            childIndent = "",
 705â†’        )
 706â†’    }
 707â†’
 708â†’    private fun visitObjectDeclaration(node: ASTNode) {
 709â†’        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 710â†’        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 711â†’        node

1046â†’    private fun visitNewLineIndentation(
1047â†’        node: ASTNode,
1048â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1049â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,

1061â†’                emit(

1064â†’                    true,
1065â†’                )
1066â†’            } else {
1067â†’                // Indentation was at correct level but contained invalid indent characters. This violation has already
1068â†’                // been emitted.
1069â†’            }
1070â†’            LOGGER.trace {
1071â†’                "Line $line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "changed indentation to ${expectedIndentation.length} " +
1072â†’                    "(from ${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}"
1073â†’            }
1074â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
1075â†’                (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(

1081â†’        }
1082â†’    }

1084â†’    private fun ASTNode.ignoreIndent(): Boolean {
1085â†’        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
1086â†’        if (text.endsWith("\n") && nextLeaf.isStartOfRawStringLiteral()) {

1088â†’            return true // raw strings (""") are allowed at column 0
1089â†’        }
1090â†’
1091â†’        nextLeaf
1092â†’            ?.parent(strict = false) { it.psi is PsiComment }
1093â†’            ?.let { comment ->
1094â†’                if (text.endsWith("\n")) {
1095â†’                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1096â†’                    return true // comments are allowed at column 0
1097â†’                }
1098â†’                if (comment.textContains('\n') && comment.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {

1100â†’                    // `/*` and `*/` can still be indented
1101â†’                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1102â†’                    return true
1103â†’                }
1104â†’            }
1105â†’        return false

1119â†’                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ->
1120â†’                    lastIndexContext.firstChildIndent
1121â†’
1122â†’                this == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ||
1123â†’                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ->
1124â†’                    lastIndexContext.lastChildIndent
1125â†’
1126â†’                else -> lastIndexContext.childIndent

1131â†’    private fun ASTNode.normalizedIndent(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit): String {
1132â†’        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1133â†’        return when (indentConfig.indentStyle) {
1134â†’            SPACE -> {
1135â†’                if ('\t' in nodeIndent) {
1136â†’                    emit(
1137â†’                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1138â†’                        "Unexpected tab character(s)",
1139â†’                        true,
1140â†’                    )
1141â†’                    indentConfig.toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndent)
1142â†’                } else {
1143â†’                    nodeIndent
1144â†’                }
1145â†’            }
1146â†’
1147â†’            TAB -> {
1148â†’                val acceptableTrailingSpaces = acceptableTrailingSpaces()
1149â†’                val nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces = nodeIndent.removeSuffix(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1150â†’                if (' ' in nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces) {
1151â†’                    emit(
1152â†’                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1153â†’                        "Unexpected space character(s)",
1154â†’                        true,
1155â†’                    )
1156â†’                    indentConfig
1157â†’                        .toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces)
1158â†’                        .plus(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1159â†’                } else {
1160â†’                    nodeIndent
1161â†’                }
1162â†’            }
1163â†’        }
1164â†’    }

1182â†’    private fun ASTNode.acceptableTrailingSpaces(): String {
1183â†’        require(elementType == WHITE_SPACE)
1184â†’        val acceptableTrailingSpaces =
1185â†’            when (nextLeaf()?.elementType) {
1186â†’                KDOC_LEADING_ASTERISK, KDOC_END -> {

1188â†’                    // style (tabs or spaces) except for the starting line of the KDoc comment

1190â†’                }
1191â†’
1192â†’                TYPE_CONSTRAINT -> {

1194â†’                    //    where A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>,

1196â†’                    TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT

1198â†’
1199â†’                else -> ""
1200â†’            }
1201â†’        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1202â†’        return if (nodeIndent.endsWith(acceptableTrailingSpaces)) {
1203â†’            return acceptableTrailingSpaces
1204â†’        } else {
1205â†’            ""
1206â†’        }
